hypothesis: If language models fine-tuned on known alloy compositions are further aligned using Direct Preference Optimization with physics-based thermodynamic simulation rewards, then they will generate novel BCC/B2 superalloy candidates with higher thermodynamic validity and phase stability than models trained only with supervised fine-tuning or baseline prompting approaches, because preference learning directly optimizes the model's generative distribution toward compositions that satisfy hierarchical materials design objectives derived from first-principles thermodynamic calculations.
